topic: European politicians facing legal action over controversial or deleted social media posts

articles.headline_en: French far-left MEP Rima Hassan detained for 15 hours over now-deleted controversial X post
articles.summary_en: French MEP Rima Hassan was detained for 15 hours on April 3, 2026, over a deleted X post quoting a perpetrator of the 1972 Lod airport attack, facing charges of online terrorism apology with a potential seven-year sentence. She denounces the action as political and judicial harassment targeting her pro-Palestinian activism. Her trial is set for July 7, 2026.
articles.significance: This case highlights tensions between freedom of expression and laws against glorifying terrorism, potentially setting a precedent for prosecuting politicians' social media activity in Europe.

articles.headline_en: Palestinian-French lawmaker arrested after praising 1972 bomber of Ben Gurion airport
articles.summary_en: On April 3, 2026, French MEP Rima Hassan was detained for several hours on charges of advocating terrorism due to a March 26 X post quoting Kozo Okamoto, a convicted terrorist from the 1972 Lod attack that killed 26 people; the post was later deleted. Prosecutors scheduled her trial for July 7, with possible penalties of seven years in prison and a €100,000 fine. The case follows complaints from antisemitism watchdogs amid her history of pro-Palestinian statements.
articles.significance: It underscores platform moderation and legal pressures on political speech related to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, raising questions about selective enforcement against far-left figures.

editorial_overview: A prominent case in France involves far-left MEP Rima Hassan detained over a deleted social media post perceived as glorifying terrorism, reflecting broader European scrutiny of politicians' online expression. No other major incidents of post deletions, controversies, or government-ordered removals by public figures were reported in Europe last week. This development signals rising legal risks for inflammatory digital speech amid geopolitical tensions.
